zh.Glamorgan, Wales: 英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根
zh.lifeSummary: Gwenllian David於大約1405年出生在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根。她於大約1434年與Ieuan ap Gwilym在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世在英國威爾斯。

zh-hans.Glamorgan, Wales: 英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Gwenllian David于大约1405年出生在英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根。她于大约1434年与Ieuan ap Gwilym在英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世在英国威尔斯。

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish, Welsh, Scottish, English, French, Breton, Portuguese, Czech, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Dávid), Slovak (mainly Dávid): from the Hebrew personal name David (in Hungarian and Slovak spelled Dávid), interpreted as ‘beloved’. The name has been perennially popular among Jews, in honor of the Biblical king of this name. His prominence, and the vivid narrative of his life contained in the First Book of Samuel, led to adoption of the name among Christians in the Middle Ages in various parts of Europe. In Britain, the popularity of this as a personal name was increased for two reasons. Firstly by virtue of its being the name of the patron saint of Wales who was abbot-bishop in the 6th century at what became known as Saint David's in Pembrokeshire. There are numerous dedications and placenames honouring the saint in south Wales, and it is no coincidence that the modern surname is heavily concentrated there, especially in Glamorgan. Secondly, the name was borne by two kings of Scotland (David I, reigning 1124–53, and David II, 1329–71). Its popularity in Russia is largely due to the fact that this was the ecclesiastical name adopted by Saint Gleb (died 1015), one of two sons of Prince Vladimir of Kiev who were martyred for their Christian zeal. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from some other languages, especially Assyrian/Chaldean Dawid and Arabic Daud (with variants, such as Daoud and Dawood ).

History: Guillaume David from France married Marie Armand in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Davi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Antonio, Guido, Rosario, Mario, Sal, Calogero, Gino, Vincenzo, Vito, Alessandra, Biagio, Biago, Carmela.
Italian (Sicily; also Davì): perhaps from an altered form of the personal name Davide (see David ).
Italian (northern, mainly Veneto): from the personal name Davio, a variant of Davino .

variants.name: Dauti
meanings: Albanian: from the Albanian Muslim personal name Daut (definite form Dauti), from Turkish Davud, an equivalent of David .

variants.name: Davey
meanings: English: from the personal name Davy, originally an Old French vernacular form of the Biblical name David which became the most common form of the name in medieval England.

variants.name: Daviau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Aime, Aldor, Emile, Normand.
French: from a derivative of Davi, a vernacular form of the personal name David . Compare Davio and Devoid .

variants.name: Davide
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Gandolfo, Marco.
Italian: from the personal name Davide, an equivalent of David .

variants.name: Dawid
meanings: Polish: from the personal name Dawid, Polish form of the Biblical name David .
Assyrian/Chaldean: from the personal name Dāwīd, Syriac form of the Biblical name David . It is often spelled in the Arabized form Daud (or Daoud and Dawood ).

variants.name: Ben-David
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Shula, Ilan, Meir, Meyer, Ari, Avi, Ayal, Haim, Limor, Menashe, Mordechai, Moshe.
Jewish (from North Africa and the Ottoman Empire; also Bendavid): patronymic from the personal name David + Hebrew or vernacular Arabic ben ‘son’.

variants.name: Daud
meanings: Arabic: from the personal name Dāwūd, Arabic form of the Biblical name David . In Islam, Dāwūd is regarded as a messenger of God/Allah. Among Christians this name is found in part as an Arabized form of the Assyrian/Chaldean variant Dawid . Compare Daoud and Dawood .

variants.name: Davidenko
meanings: Ukrainian (standard transliteration Davydenko): patronymic from the personal name Davyd (see David ), formed with the diminutive suffix -enko.

variants.name: Dacus
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of the personal name Deicws, a pet form of Dafydd, Welsh form of David . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Gwenllian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: from gwen, the feminine form of gwyn ‘white, fair; blessed, holy’ ( see Gwen ) + lliant ‘flood, flow’ (probably in the transferred sense ‘foamy’ or ‘white’, referring to a pale complexion).

en.lifeSummary: Gwenllian David was born about 1405, in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom. She married Ieuan ap Gwilym about 1434, in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in Wales, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Gwenllian David est née environ 1405, à Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Ieuan ap Gwilym environ 1434, à Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Gwenllian David wurde um 1405 in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat um 1434 in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom Ieuan ap Gwilym geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in Wales, United Kingdom.

ht.Glamorgan, Wales: Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

it.lifeSummary: Gwenllian David è nata il circa 1405 nel Glamorgan, Galles, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Ieuan ap Gwilym circa 1434, nel Glamorgan, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel Galles, Regno Unito.

ja.Glamorgan, Wales: イギリスウェールズグラモーガン
ja.lifeSummary: Gwenllian Davidは約1405年にイギリスウェールズグラモーガンで生まれた。約1434年にイギリスウェールズグラモーガンでIeuan ap Gwilymと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女はイギリスウェールズで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gwenllian David(은)는 대략 1405년 영국 웨일스 글램르건에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1434년 영국 웨일스 글램르건에서 Ieuan ap Gwilym(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 웨일스에서 사망했다.

mn.Glamorgan, Wales: Teyrnas Glywysing, Уэльс, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Gwenllian David nasceu aproximadamente 1405, em Glamorgan, Gales, United Kingdom. Ela casou-se com Ieuan ap Gwilym aproximadamente 1434, em Glamorgan, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em Gales, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Gwenllian David родилась около 1405 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ieuan ap Gwilym около 1434 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в местности Уэльс, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Gwenllian David nació aproximadamente 1405, en Glamorgan, Gales, Reino Unido. Se casó con Ieuan ap Gwilym aproximadamente 1434, en Glamorgan, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en Gales, Reino Unido.
